title: Preparation of samples for constituent analysis
abstract: A device for the preparation of samples for constituent analysis is provided. The device comprises a conductive body has a first face and a second face separated by a gap. A channel for containing a sample runs through the gap. The conductive body is arranged to produce, upon application of microwaves, parallel E-field lines extending in a first direction in a region of the gap. The channel runs in a second direction in the region and the angle between the first and second directions is not more than 20 degrees. The electric field transfers energy to the sample.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A device for rupturing bacteria, the device comprising: a conductive body comprising a first face and a second face, wherein the first and second faces are separated by a gap; an input for introducing a microwave signal to the conductive body; and a channel for containing a sample; wherein: the conductive body is arranged to produce, upon application of the microwave signal at the input, parallel E-field lines extending in a first direction in a region of the gap; the channel runs in a second direction in the region; and the angle between the first and second directions is not more than 45 degrees. 2. A device according to claim 1, wherein the angle is not more than 10 degrees. 3. A device according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the angle is substantially zero. 4. A device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the microwaves are applied at a resonant frequency of the device. 5. A device according to any of the proceeding claims, wherein the conductive body is a conductive ring that is split to provide said gap. 6. A device according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the conductive body is hollow and encloses a space, and the body protrudes into the space to provide the gap. 7. A device according to claim 6, wherein the body comprises first and second parts that can be releasably assembled to define the space. 8. A device according to claim 7 wherein the first part carries a protrusion that extends into the space to define the gap. 9. A device according to any of the preceding claims wherein the channel extends into the body to reach said region. 10. A device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein at least a portion of the part of the channel within said region runs in a third direction that is not parallel to the second direction. 11. A device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the channel is defined by a tube. 12. A device according to claim 11, wherein the sample is a fluid and flows through the tube. 13. A device according to any of the proceeding claims, wherein the channel is a microfluidic channel. 14. A method of rupturing bacteria using a device, the method comprising: applying a microwave signal to a conductive body via an input, the conductive body comprising a first face and a second face, wherein the first and second faces are separated by a gap; wherein, due to the microwave signal, parallel E-field lines extend in a first direction in a region of the gap; the method further comprises inserting a sample into a channel, wherein the channel runs in a second direction in the region and the angle between the first and second directions is not more than 20 degrees. 15. A method according to claim 14, wherein the angle is not more than 10 degrees. 16. A method according to claim 14 or claim 15, wherein the angle is substantially zero. 17. A method according to any of claims 14 to 16, wherein the microwaves are applied at a resonant frequency of the device. 18. A method according to any of claims 14 to 17, wherein the conductive body is a conductive ring that is split to provide said gap. 19. A method according to any of claims 14 to 17, wherein the conductive body is hollow and encloses a space, and the body protrudes into the space to provide the gap. 20. A method according to claim 19, wherein the body comprises first and second parts that can be releasably assembled to define the space. 21. A method according to claim 20 wherein the first part carries a protrusion that extends into the space to define the gap. 22. A method according to any of claims 14 to 21, wherein the channel extends into the body to reach said region. 23. A method according to any of claims 14 to 22, wherein at least a portion of the part of the channel within said region runs in a third direction that is not parallel to the second direction. 24. A method according to any of claims 14 to 23, wherein the sample is fluid and wherein inserting the sample into a channel comprises moving the sample through the channel. 25. A method according to any of claims 14 to 24, comprising pumping the sample through the channel until the volume of the sample has passed through the channel and been exposed to the E-field. 26. A method according to any of claims 14 to 25, wherein the channel is defined by a tube. 27. A method according to any of claims 14 to 26, wherein the channel is a microfluidic channel.
